title: Numeric measurement + unit selector pairs render side-by-side with w2 width class
description: Numeric measurement fields paired with unit selectors (height + height units, weight + weight units, temperature + temperature units, dose + dose units, etc.) render side-by-side using OpenClinica's w2 width-class appearance modifier. AUTO-DETECTION (conservative): the skill applies the pattern when (a) a decimal or integer field is immediately followed by a select_one field, AND (b) the select_one field's list_name matches unit*, *_U, *_UNIT, *_UNITS, OR the select_one field's name ends in _U, _UNIT, _UNITS. AUTO-APPLIED BEHAVIOR: the numeric field gets appearance=w2; the unit field gets appearance='horizontal w2' (preserves any existing horizontal from §17/§18 layout rules). UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0. Override: customer library uses different layout (stacked, or w3 width class); carry forward as-authored.
rationale: A height value without its unit is ambiguous: 72 could be inches, cm, or kg-equivalents. Visual pairing prevents data-entry errors where the operator enters a value and forgets to set the unit (or vice versa). The pair reads as one composite measurement (72.5 inches) instead of stacked rows. The w2 width class allocates half the form width to each field, making the side-by-side relationship visually unambiguous. The conservative auto-detection (immediate adjacency + unit-naming pattern) prevents false positives.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} (${field.type}) is immediately followed by a unit select_one; auto-applied appearance=w2 for side-by-side rendering. The unit select_one should also get appearance='horizontal w2'. Customer-library overrides preserved.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §26 during Phase B.1d. Structured detection (has_sibling with next_in_form scope from B.1c-1, plus matches on list_name / name unit-naming patterns). The ensure directive sets THIS field's appearance to w2; the matching update on the next field's appearance (to 'horizontal w2') requires applying an effect to a different entity than the matched one — a DSL gap from B.1c. Soft directive carries the next-field update guidance. A future 'effect_on_sibling' or 'set_at_path' with computed paths would promote this to fully-structured.

Corrected kind from 'structured' to 'hybrid' during B.1d validation pass. The ensure directive sets field.appearance=w2 on the matched field (valid structured work); the soft directive described updating the immediately-following unit field's appearance, which requires applying effects to a sibling entity — a known DSL gap not yet addressed. Schema forbids ensure + soft coexisting. Resolution: keep ensure (the structured part lands properly), drop soft, and document the sibling-update gap in the flag's message. When 'effect_on_sibling' or similar lands as an effect feature, the matching update on the next-field appearance can promote back to structured.
